name: webhooks
context: Configure Linear webhooks for real-time event-driven automation on issues, projects, and comments
info: Linear webhooks push real-time notifications when resources change. Supported resources include issues, comments, projects, project updates, labels, cycles, documents, initiatives, customers, and users. Each event includes an action (create, update, remove), the full entity data, the actor who made the change, and for updates, the previous values. Webhooks can target all public teams or a single team. Security is enforced via HMAC-SHA256 signature verification using the webhook signing secret. Agents use this skill to build automated triage bots, sync Linear state to external systems, trigger CI/CD pipelines on issue events, and post notifications to Slack or other channels. See [[linear/auth]] for authentication and [[linear/issues]] for the primary webhook target.
triggers: react to issue changes in linear

sections.title: Creating webhooks
sections.body: Create webhooks via the `webhookCreate` mutation or Linear Settings → API → Webhooks. Specify `url` (HTTPS endpoint), `resourceTypes` (array of resource names), optional `teamId` (scope to one team), and `label`. The response includes the webhook `id`, `url`, `enabled` status, and `secret` for signature verification. Omit `teamId` to receive events for all public teams.

sections.title: Event types and payloads
sections.body: Events have `action` (create/update/remove), `type` (resource name), `data` (full entity), `actor` (who triggered it), `createdAt`, and `webhookTimestamp`. For updates, `updatedFrom` contains previous field values. Headers include `Linear-Delivery` (UUID), `Linear-Event` (type), and `Linear-Signature` (HMAC).

sections.title: Signature verification
sections.body: Compute HMAC-SHA256 of the raw request body using the webhook signing secret. Compare against the `Linear-Signature` header using constant-time comparison (`timingSafeEqual`) to prevent timing attacks. Also verify `webhookTimestamp` is within 60 seconds of current time to prevent replay attacks. Optionally allowlist Linear's IP addresses.

sections.title: Delivery requirements and retries
sections.body: Endpoints must be publicly accessible over HTTPS, respond with HTTP 200 within 5 seconds. Failed deliveries retry at 1 minute, 1 hour, then 6 hours. Persistent failures cause automatic webhook disabling. Check Linear Settings → API → Webhooks for delivery status and error logs.
